pages.text: $(o)"Make mana flow like water."$()$(br2)The $(l)Mana Spreader$() is the core of long-range mana transmission — pulls from adjacent reservoirs, projects toward a bound target.

pages.title: Mechanics
pages.text: Internal buffer: $(l)256$() mana.$(br2)Every $(l)10 ticks$() (0.5s):$(br)· Pull mana from $(l)mana-emitting$() neighbors (reservoirs etc.)$(br)· Push $(l)16$() mana toward the bound target$(br2)Target must be within $(l)16 blocks$() and accept mana (e.g. Circle Core).

pages.title: Binding
pages.text: 1. Hold a $(l)Bound Aether Pearl$() and $(l)Shift + Right-click$() the target block (e.g. Circle Core). Coordinates are recorded — chat confirms.$(br2)2. With the recorded pearl, right-click the spreader.$(br2)Once bound, the spreader pushes mana with a visible particle trail.

pages.title: Applications
pages.text: $(l)Remote power$(): keep reservoirs in a vault, spread to a distant core.$(br2)$(l)Multi-core sharing$(): one reservoir array + multiple spreaders → power many circles in parallel.$(br2)$(l)Around-the-corner$(): unlike rune conduits, spreaders work across walls and structures — line-of-sight not even required, only range.
